checks.finding: Every AC-NNN cited in tasks.md (AC-001..AC-018) resolves to a matching row in acceptance-tests.md; grep-diff of AC IDs in both files is identical.

All four `### Blockers` blocks use `None` or comma-separated `T-\d{3}` lists (T-001: None; T-002: T-001; T-003: T-001, T-002; T-004: T-001, T-002, T-003); precheck-result.json confirms blockers_format_valid: true.
REQ-001..REQ-006 each appear in a task Requirements field (T-001: REQ-001/005/006; T-002: REQ-002/005/006; T-003: REQ-003/005/006; T-004: REQ-004/005/006).
AC-001..AC-018 each appear in a task's Done When list with explicit test-ID/AC citations (e.g. AC-018 in T-004 TEST-018); none are uncovered.
Every task (T-001..T-004) declares a Requirements field with at least one REQ-NNN; no orphan tasks.

No task titles are pure test-writing tasks distinct from a Done-When AC citation; all four tasks already cite explicit AC-NNN IDs throughout their Done When lists, so no task is orphaned from acceptance criteria.
All four tasks declare Approval: Draft (not Approved) and Status: Planned, valid for round 2 per the state rules.
All four tasks declare Risk: medium / Required Workflow: acceptance-first, matching the risk-gate-matrix pairing; precheck-result.json records workflow_match_precheck: PASS.

AC-001..013 and AC-018 each map to exactly one task (traceability.md Acceptance Mapping). AC-014/015/016/017 are claimed by all four tasks, but each task's Done-When bullet explicitly scopes its own instance ('for this task's twins', 'AC-016 share for this task', named per-suite leg for AC-017), matching the per-suite/per-task grain the ACs themselves define; no undifferentiated duplicate primary targets.
Every Blockers reference (T-001, T-002, T-003) exists in tasks.md and in dependency-graph.json's nodes array [T-001, T-002, T-003, T-004].

BLOCKERS-FORMAT is PASS, so this check ran. dependency-graph.json edges (T-002->T-001, T-003->T-001, T-003->T-002, T-004->T-001, T-004->T-002, T-004->T-003) form a linear DAG with no revisited node; no cycle.

T-001/T-002/T-003 titles use 'and' to join a deliverable with directly-tied verification work (its own registration-forcing/smoke/RED-differential evidence for that same suite); T-004 uses 'with', not 'and'. Scope sections likewise only combine sub-steps of one coherent per-task deliverable (REQ-004 explicitly bundles the escalation leg and parity extension into one suite/issue in requirements.md and design.md's placement decision).

The round-1 vague REQ-006 clause is fixed: each task's Done-When item now states 'require NO update for this task — verified: none references the surfaces this task touches ... and tests/validate-repository.sh exits 0', naming a concrete command and inspectable claim. All other Done-When items name concrete artifacts/commands (TEST-NNN proving/greping specific files, green.log/red-differential.log, quality-gate PASS verdict); no forbidden vague verbs ('ensure', 'consider', 'works correctly', etc.) found.

T-001..T-004 all appear in traceability.md's Task Mapping and Acceptance Mapping tables; REQ-001..REQ-006 in traceability.md all exist in requirements.md. Layer Spec cells cite real headings (infra-spec.md#cicd-sequence/#runtime-dependencies/#deployment-topology/#runtime-budget; security-spec.md#trust-boundaries/#security-tests/#authorization) or a reasoned N/A for REQ-006, and the cited layer docs' hashes match the manifest/precheck layer_sha256 values.
